title: Serial Monitor Deep Dive
subtitle: see, debug, communicate!
overview: This session helps students understand how the Arduino Serial Monitor works, how computers communicate with microcontrollers, and how to use Serial Monitor for debugging, sensor readings, and user interaction.

images._note: Add real classroom screenshots, Arduino IDE photos, sensor outputs, and student activities.

activity.title: Live Temperature Monitor using Serial Monitor
steps: Connect Arduino to computer using USB cable
Open Serial Monitor from Tools menu
Observe live data appearing on screen
Change baud rate and see communication issues
Challenge: Display sensor values every second

quiz.q: What is the main use of Serial Monitor?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor helps Arduino and computer exchange information.
9600 is the baud rate or communication speed.

quiz.q: Which function prints text with a new line?
quiz.explanation: Serial.println() moves output to the next line.

quiz.q: Where can you open Serial Monitor in Arduino IDE?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor is available inside the Tools menu.

quiz.q: What happens if baud rates do not match?
quiz.explanation: Wrong baud rates create unreadable communication.

quiz.q: Why is Serial Monitor useful in projects?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor helps identify errors and monitor values.

quiz.q: Which device sends data to the computer?
quiz.explanation: Arduino sends live data through serial communication.

quiz.q: Which symbol cable is mostly used for Arduino communication?
quiz.explanation: USB cable is commonly used for programming and communication.

quiz.q: What can be displayed in Serial Monitor?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor can display text, numbers, and sensor data.
Debugging means finding and solving problems in code.

quiz.q: Which function prints text without moving to next line?
quiz.explanation: Serial.print() keeps printing on the same line.

quiz.q: What type of data can Arduino send to Serial Monitor?
quiz.explanation: Arduino can send many types of information.

quiz.q: Which shortcut opens Serial Monitor quickly?
quiz.explanation: Ctrl + Shift + M opens Serial Monitor in Arduino IDE.

quiz.q: Why do students use Serial Monitor in robotics?
options: To test sensors and robot behavior
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor helps students understand how robots and sensors work in real time.
